title: OSC 8 hyperlinks — make file paths in tool headers clickable
task:
Modern terminals (Ghostty, iTerm2, Kitty, WezTerm, Alacritty) support OSC 8 hyperlinks: `\x1b]8;;file:///abs/path\x1b\\text\x1b]8;;\x1b\\` renders `text` as a clickable link to the file URL. Make every file path that appears in a tool header / body clickable when the terminal supports it.

OUTCOME — clicking the path in `~ /Users/tom/foo.rs  +5 -3 lines  (0.42s)` opens the file in the configured editor. Fallback is unchanged (plain path text) on terminals without OSC 8 support.

requirements.description: Add `osc8_links: bool` capability detection to TerminalCaps (crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/caps.rs). The detection logic varies — most modern terms advertise via $TERM or $TERM_PROGRAM. Conservative approach: enable when TERM_PROGRAM is in {iTerm.app, WezTerm, ghostty, kitty} OR when $TERMINFO advertises specific entries. When unknown, default to off (safer — broken OSC 8 produces visible garbage on non-supporting terms).
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: caps with TERM_PROGRAM='ghostty' has osc8_links=true. Unknown terminal has osc8_links=false.

requirements.description: Add a helper `wrap_osc8_link(text: &str, url: &str, caps: &TerminalCaps) -> String` in crates/norn-tui/src/render/style.rs. When caps.osc8_links is true, returns `\x1b]8;;{url}\x1b\\{text}\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`. When false, returns text.to_string(). The URL must be a file:// URL; the helper takes the text + URL and assembles correctly.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: with osc8_links=true and (text='foo.rs', url='file:///abs/foo.rs'), output contains both the OSC 8 open and close sequences. With osc8_links=false, output is just 'foo.rs'.

requirements.description:
Apply to file paths in tool headers. The paths that should become clickable:
- EditRenderer header (`~ {path}` → wrap path in OSC 8)
- ApplyPatchRenderer header (`patch {first_path}` → wrap)
- WriteRenderer header (`+ {path}` → wrap)
- ReadRenderer header (`> {path}` → wrap)
- SearchRenderer body (each `{path}` heading per group → wrap)
- LspRenderer body locations (each `{path}:{line}:{col}` → wrap with file:///abs/path#L{line})
Keep the rest of the header (stats, duration, etc.) unwrapped — they're not navigation targets.
requirements.acceptance: Unit tests for each renderer verify that with osc8_links=true the header contains the OSC 8 sequences around the path, and with osc8_links=false the header is unchanged.

requirements.description: Handle path-to-URL conversion safely. Relative paths in the tool result must be resolved to absolute (otherwise file:// URL is meaningless). The TUI knows its working directory (via state.working_dir if present, or via env::current_dir() as a fallback). Add a helper `path_to_file_url(path: &str, base: &Path) -> String` that returns `file:///{absolute_path}` with proper URL encoding for special characters.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: a relative path 'src/foo.rs' with base='/Users/tom/project' produces 'file:///Users/tom/project/src/foo.rs'. An absolute path passes through unchanged.

requirements.description: Gates pass: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files returns zero.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/caps.rs — TerminalCaps. Add the osc8_links field.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/style.rs — add wrap_osc8_link helper.
- All the tool renderers (rich/*.rs, compact.rs) — wrap path emissions.

OSC 8 SPEC: https://gist.github.com/egmontkob/eb114294efbcd5adb1944c9f3cb5feda
Format: `\x1b]8;{id};{url}\x1b\\{text}\x1b]8;;\x1b\\`. The `id` field is for hover-effect grouping; omit (just `;;`). Terminator is ST (\x1b\\), some terminals also accept BEL (\x07).

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — don't reinvent:
- The TerminalCaps detection machinery — add the new field alongside existing ones.
- The path field extraction in each renderer (string_field helper).

DESIGN TASTE: hyperlinks are invisible to the eye on terminals that support them (no visual underline by default unless the terminal chooses to), so the link is a CAPABILITY ENHANCEMENT — text reads the same; click works when it works. No visual change for users on non-supporting terminals.

STANDARDS: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; files under 500 LOC.
